label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why the visitor came to John's house. The options are to steal something, read the story, have a drink, or visit the writer. So first, I need to look at the details given in the story. The visitor broke into the house, had a drink, smoked some cigarettes, and read the story. Then left a note. The note says, "I have read your story and I don't think it is very good. Please read my suggestions and you can finish it. By the way, I am a thief. I'm not going to steal anything tonight. But if you become a successful writer, I will return." Now, the thief mentions he's a thief but didn't steal anything that night. His main action was reading the story and giving suggestions. The other actions (drink and smoke) might be just what the thief did, but the main purpose seems to be related to the story. Because the note starts by mentioning reading the story and giving feedback. The question is about the reason (in order to) for the visit. Even though the thief did have a drink and smoke, those might just be actions during the visit, not the main purpose. The thief specifically mentions reading the story in the note. Also, the story says that the thief left suggestions to help John finish his story. Therefore, the main purpose was to read the story and give feedback. But looking at the options, option B is "read the story." So that would be the answer. Option
